mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series 316d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 316d engine typically costs £2,800–£5,500 supply only, with the B47 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. A snapped timing chain on an N47 can cost approximately £7,000 for a full engine replacement.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series 316d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine era your 316d has. The E90 (2009–2011) and pre-LCI F30 (2012–2015) with the N47 have the well-documented rear-mounted timing chain risk — a known weakness that can cause catastrophic failure. The LCI F30 (2015–2019) B47 is significantly more reliable — the chain was relocated to the front. Both N47 and B47 share EGR cooler issues, subject to a BMW recall.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series 316d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (cold-start rattle is the warning sign), EGR cooler leaks affecting both N47 and B47 (subject to a BMW recall), and DPF clogging exacerbated by short journeys. The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 316d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept £5,000–£12,000 F30 3 Series, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 30–80% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a cherished example. Against a tired £3,500 example, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series 316d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: E90 (2009–2011) 316d engines carry the N47D16C code. Pre-LCI F30/F31 (2012–2015) use the N47D20C code. LCI F30/F31 (2015–2019) use the B47D20A code. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation. If your car is registered after 2015, it's almost certainly the B47.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,500+ engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B47 has far fewer timing chain issues — but regular oil changes remain essential.

eraMap.title: The 316d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Rear-mounted timing chain risk — cold-start rattle is the warning sign
2.0L N47 — same rear-chain design; timing chain remains the critical weakness
B47 replaced N47 at LCI — chain relocated to front; meaningfully improved. EGR cooler recall applies

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current 316d replacement costs. The B47 variant typically sits at the higher end of the range. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 316d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 316d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-LCI F30/F31 (2015+) with B47 engine (significantly reduced chain risk)
* Documented timing chain history on any N47-engined car
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
avoidIf: * E90 or pre-LCI F30 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* No service history — the N47 is unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss — EGR cooler may be failing

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 316d is BMW's entry-level 2.0-litre diesel across the E90 and F30 3 Series generations. If you're looking at an E90 or pre-2015 F30, it has the N47 — with the well-known rear-mounted chain risk. The 2015-on LCI B47 is a significant improvement, fixing the chain design. Budget for chain maintenance if the history is unclear. With typical F30 values of £5,000–£12,000, a £4,000–£5,000 engine replacement is a significant but often worthwhile investment.

cta.label: → Compare 316d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 3 Series 316d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (N47 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47D16, N47D20C or B47D20A) and oil-change discipline
* EGR Recall — Confirm recall completion with BMW (both N47 and B47 affected)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
